methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Axelera AI is a Series B chip design company competing in the semiconductors sector alongside 8 private peers (Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, DEEPX and others) and 3 public comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The company has raised $200M+ to date.

Two valuation methods triangulate on Axelera AI's worth. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $200M total raised proxy, implying $380M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage private companies yields a $1.0B median valuation, with a range of $500M to $5.0B anchored by Etched at $5.0B (Series A) and DEEPX at $529M (Series C). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the sector P/S median is 24.2x across the three public tickers, but Axelera AI's revenue is not publicly disclosed, as is typical for private companies.

These two methods support a valuation range of $323M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $690M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around commercialization trajectory and near-term revenue visibility, both critical for semiconductor hardware startups. The peer-median method carries greater weight given the direct comparability of stage and sector, though the high variance within the peer set ($500M–$5.0B) indicates significant heterogeneity in investor expectations across the frontier-chip space.
